steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Find an expression equivalent to $7x^3+56x^2$ by factoring out the greatest common factor.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $x^3(7+56x)$: distributes to $7x^3+56x^4$, not the original. ✗
  • Choice B — $7x^2(x+49)$: distributes to $7x^3+343x^2$; the constant $49$ is wrong because $56\div7=8$, not $49$. ✗
  • Choice C — $7x^2(x+8)$: distributes to $7x^3+56x^2$. ✓
  • Choice D — $x^2(7+56x)$: distributes to $7x^2+56x^3$, which swaps the exponents. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
